answer.content:
Decision Trees - Interactive Playground

Extremely minimal interface. Use controls to generate data, build and edit trees, step through training, evaluate, traverse and export/import. Many controls and workflows are provided to explore decision tree behavior.

1) Data Generation & Dataset Controls
2) Tree Builder (Auto & Step-by-step)
3) Manual Node Editing & Operations
4) Evaluation & Traversal

Single-sample traversal

Dataset (raw & interactive)
Tree View & Interaction
Step-by-step Training Inspector
Logs & Console

messages.content:
Generate a single, self-contained HTML file with embedded CSS and JavaScript that demonstrates the concept Decision Trees with MAXIMUM interactivity and rich user control. Focus on:

- Multiple interactive controls (buttons, sliders, input fields)
- Complex state transitions and user-driven workflows
- Deep exploration capabilities with many possible interaction paths
- Rich functionality as the PRIMARY goal

IMPORTANT: Keep visual design EXTREMELY MINIMAL and BASIC - use simple black text on white background, basic HTML elements with no styling, no colors, no animations. The interface should be functional but visually plain and unpolished. Prioritize interactive richness over visual appeal.

Only respond with the complete HTML file.
